description: Figure 2 presents S-N curves (stress amplitude versus number of cycles to failure) for Fe-Mn-Al-C steel under three distinct testing conditions: (1) room temperature (293 K, diamonds), (2) elevated temperature (673 K, circles), and (3) thermal cycling between 293 K and 673 K (triangles). The y-axis displays applied stress amplitude in MPa (220–540 MPa range) and the x-axis shows number of cycles N on a logarithmic scale (0.1–50 × 10⁶). Curve 2 (673 K) exhibits higher fatigue strength than Curve 1 (293 K), indicating anomalous temperature dependence where elevated temperature improves fatigue resistance. The thermocycling Curve 3 occupies an intermediate position between the isothermal tests. Horizontal arrows denote run-out specimens that did not fail within the tested cycle limits. This data demonstrates that Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys exhibit superior fatigue resistance at 673 K compared to room temperature, supporting the conclusion that time-dependent deformation aging in microplastic deformation zones enhances high-temperature fatigue performance, and that components can successfully withstand cyclic elasto-plastic loading under periodic temperature changes.
